SAP Future of PMM Training
Chapter 2 Learning Guide
futureofpmm.com

Visibility in the Age of Agents

Key concepts and takeaways from Chapter 2

Learning Objectives

  • Understand how AI agents discover and rank vendors
  • Learn the difference between SEO and GEO
  • Build structured content that LLMs can parse and cite
  • Test and measure your AI visibility

The Three Layers of Agent Discovery

LayerWhat It IsHow to Influence
Training DataKnowledge baked into the modelAuthoritative content, major publications, Wikipedia
Real-Time RetrievalRAG pulls from live sourcesGEO tactics, current website, updated docs
Entity RelationshipsKnowledge graph connectionsWikidata, consistent naming, partner/customer mentions

SEO vs GEO

SEO (Old)GEO (New)
Optimize for keywordsOptimize for entity recognition
Build backlinksBuild knowledge graph presence
Drive clicks to websiteGet cited in AI responses
Compete for SERP positionCompete for mention quality

The GEO Playbook

  • Schema.org Markup — Structured data that tells agents what your content means
  • Entity Establishment — Wikipedia, Wikidata, consistent naming
  • Quotable Claims — Specific, standalone, unique, verifiable statements
  • Authoritative Linking — Citations from recognized sources

The Quotability Test

For each key claim, ask:

  • Is it specific? Numbers and facts beat superlatives
  • Is it standalone? Makes sense out of context
  • Is it unique? Competitor can't say the same thing
  • Is it verifiable? Can be checked against other sources
Before: "We're the leading platform with best-in-class capabilities."
After: "SAP Datasphere connects to 150+ data sources and processes 2 billion transactions daily."

AI Visibility Audit Checklist

  1. Query 3 AI tools (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude) about your category
  2. Document: Are you mentioned? How are you described? What competitors appear?
  3. Check citations (where visible)
  4. Identify gaps: Where should you appear but don't?
  5. Create action items to improve visibility

Key Takeaways

  1. AI agents discover vendors through training data, real-time retrieval, and entity relationships
  2. GEO is the new discipline — structured content that agents can parse and cite
  3. Schema.org markup and entity establishment are your GEO foundation
  4. Quotable content must be specific, standalone, unique, and verifiable
  5. Regular AI visibility audits should be standard practice
